symptom: a fall that looked like nothing then hours of getting slowly more confused
hook: there was a crescent of blood hugging the curve of the skull and shoving the brain sideways
whatYouSee: A crescent shaped collection of blood hugs the curve of the skull and pushes the brain away from it.
whyItMatters: A bridging vein between the brain and the skull has torn and the growing blood collection is now squeezing the brain sideways.
treatment: Small ones are watched closely with repeat scans while larger ones need urgent surgical drainage to relieve the pressure.
takeaway: A crescent moon shape hugging the skull is a subdural bleed and the story often starts with an unremarkable looking fall.
laypersonQuestion: Does one side look more squashed than the other?
seedHint: Compare the two sides and look at the shape hugging the bone.

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in after a fall that looked like nothing then hours of getting slowly more confused.

Then the CT scan loaded 😭

And there was a crescent of blood hugging the curve of the skull and shoving the brain sideways.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Acute subdural hematoma

👀 What you see:
A crescent shaped collection of blood hugs the curve of the skull and pushes the brain away from it.

🦴 Why it matters:
A bridging vein between the brain and the skull has torn and the growing blood collection is now squeezing the brain sideways.

💊 Treatment:
Small ones are watched closely with repeat scans while larger ones need urgent surgical drainage to relieve the pressure.
generated.ctaText:
If you like guessing what is wrong on an X-ray wait until you see it on a CT scan.

I put 10 real CT cases into Weird CT Case Files: Volume 1.

Look then guess then flip for the breakdown.

Grab it here 👇🏼
